# AADNet Training Commands (GCP VM)

## Machine profile used for this runbook
- VM: g2-standard-8
- CPU/RAM: 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM
- GPU: 1 x NVIDIA L4 (CUDA)

## Recommended runtime tuning for this machine
# Keep CPU thread usage bounded to avoid dataloader thrash on 8 vCPUs.
export OMP_NUM_THREADS=8
export MKL_NUM_THREADS=8
export NUMEXPR_MAX_THREADS=8

# For AADNet configs, set num_workers in YAML to 4-6 for this VM.
# Start with 4 if memory pressure is high, otherwise use 6.

## 1) Go to project and activate virtual environment
cd ~/training/AAD_XAI
source .venv/bin/activate

## 2) Install dependencies required by AADNet scripts
python -m pip install --upgrade pip
python -m pip install -r requirements.txt
python -m pip install pyyaml mne-bids pandas seaborn

## 3) Set DTU dataset path (local copy on VM)
export DATASET=$PWD/aad_data/datasets/DTU
echo $DATASET
ls $DATASET/eeg_new | head
ls $DATASET/Audio | head

## 3.1) Update dataloader workers for this machine (recommended)
# Adjust both SI and SS configs to use 6 workers on g2-standard-8.
sed -i 's/^\(\s*num_workers:\s*\).*/\16/' external/AADNet/config/config_AADNet_SI_DTU.yml
sed -i 's/^\(\s*num_workers:\s*\).*/\16/' external/AADNet/config/config_AADNet_SS_DTU.yml

# Verify the values were applied.
grep -n "num_workers" external/AADNet/config/config_AADNet_SI_DTU.yml
grep -n "num_workers" external/AADNet/config/config_AADNet_SS_DTU.yml

## 4) Optional: verify GPU while training (run in another terminal)
watch -n 2 nvidia-smi

## 5) Move into AADNet folder
cd external/AADNet

## 6) First run: LOSO training (recommended to start here)
python cross_validate_loso.py -c config/config_AADNet_SI_DTU.yml -j LOSO_AADNet_DTU_retry

## 7) Full subject-specific training
python cross_validate_ss.py -c config/config_AADNet_SS_DTU.yml -j SS_AADNet_DTU_retry

## 8) Verify local outputs/results were created
cd ~/training/AAD_XAI
ls -ltr external/AADNet/output | tail -n 30
ls -ltr external/AADNet/results | tail -n 50

## 9) (Optional) Upload training outputs/results to bucket
export ARTIFACTS_BUCKET_URI=gs://aad_data/artifacts/aad_xai
export RUN_ID=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
gsutil -m rsync -r external/AADNet/output ${ARTIFACTS_BUCKET_URI}/${RUN_ID}/aadnet_output
gsutil -m rsync -r external/AADNet/results ${ARTIFACTS_BUCKET_URI}/${RUN_ID}/aadnet_results
gsutil ls ${ARTIFACTS_BUCKET_URI}/${RUN_ID}/**

## 10) If upload fails with 403, run auth once and retry uploads
gcloud auth login
gcloud config set pass_credentials_to_gsutil true
gcloud auth application-default login

## 11) If training fails with missing module, install and retry
# examples:
python -m pip install pyyaml
python -m pip install mne-bids

## 12) If data loading feels too slow on this machine
# 1) keep dataset local on VM disk (already done under aad_data/datasets/DTU)
# 2) reduce workers to 4 if system is swapping
# 3) monitor CPU+GPU in parallel
#    - terminal A: watch -n 2 nvidia-smi
#    - terminal B: top

## 13) Run AADNet ONLY with a log file (so tail -f works)
# Run this from ~/training/AAD_XAI.
# This skips the aad_xai TRF stage entirely and only runs AADNet LOSO.
cd ~/training/AAD_XAI
mkdir -p logs
nohup bash -lc '
  source .venv/bin/activate
  export DATASET=$PWD/aad_data/datasets/DTU
  export OMP_NUM_THREADS=8
  export MKL_NUM_THREADS=8
  export NUMEXPR_MAX_THREADS=8
  cd external/AADNet
  python cross_validate_loso.py \
    -c config/config_AADNet_SI_DTU.yml \
    -j LOSO_AADNet_DTU
' > ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.log 2>&1 &
echo $! > ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.pid
echo "Training started — PID: $(cat ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.pid)"

## 14) Monitor the running training

# NOTE: "nohup: ignoring input" printed immediately after launch is NORMAL.
# It means nohup detached stdin. Training is running in the background.

# NOTE: If you ran the nohup command twice by accident, kill the first PID:
#   kill <first_pid>
# Then confirm only one python process remains:
pgrep -af "cross_validate_loso.py"

# Wait ~10 s for Python imports to finish, then check the log:
sleep 10 && tail -30 ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.log

# Follow the live log output:
tail -f ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.log

# Check that the process is still alive:
ps -fp "$(cat ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.pid)"

# If the log is still empty after 15 s, print the full log to check for errors:
cat ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.log

# Watch GPU utilisation every 2 s (open a second terminal for this):
watch -n 2 nvidia-smi

## 15) Stop training early (if needed)
kill "$(cat ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.pid)"

## 16) Get average LOSO accuracy after training completes
# Quick grep from the log — prints all accuracy/mean lines:
grep -Ei "average|mean.*acc|acc.*mean|overall.*acc|accuracy" \
     ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.log | tail -n 30

# Parse JSON result files and print per-window mean accuracy
cd ~/training/AAD_XAI
python - <<'EOF'
import json, pathlib, statistics

result_dir = pathlib.Path("external/AADNet/results")
for jf in sorted(result_dir.glob("*.json")):
    try:
        data = json.loads(jf.read_text())
        accs = []
        # try common key patterns in AADNet result files
        for key in ("accuracy", "acc", "test_accuracy", "test_acc"):
            if key in data:
                v = data[key]
                accs = v if isinstance(v, list) else [v]
                break
        if accs:
            print(f"{jf.name}: mean={statistics.mean(accs):.4f}  n={len(accs)}")
        else:
            print(f"{jf.name}: keys={list(data.keys())[:8]}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"{jf.name}: error — {e}")
EOF
